task_completion.description: Agent accesses the Splunk web interface and navigates to the Search Macros settings page.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Splunk Web UI (localhost:8000) with the 'Search Macros' page open (usually under Settings -> Advanced Search).

task_completion.description: Agent creates the 'auth_failures_by_ip(1)' macro with the correct index and logic.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A macro creation/edit screen showing the name 'auth_failures_by_ip(1)' (or 'auth_failures_by_ip' with 1 argument) and a definition that includes 'index=security_logs', failed authentication criteria, and a count function.

task_completion.description: Agent creates the 'top_attackers(1)' macro with the correct index and logic.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A macro creation/edit screen showing the name 'top_attackers(1)' (or 'top_attackers' with 1 argument) and a definition that includes 'index=security_logs', failed authentication criteria, and a command like 'top' to return top N source IPs.

task_completion.description: Agent creates the 'event_volume_trend' macro with the 'timechart' command and multiple indexes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A macro creation/edit screen showing the name 'event_volume_trend' (0 arguments) and a definition containing the 'timechart' command and referencing at least two of: 'security_logs', 'web_logs', or 'system_logs'.

task_completion.description: All three macros are saved and their status is set to enabled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Splunk Search Macros list view displaying the three newly created macros ('auth_failures_by_ip', 'top_attackers', 'event_volume_trend') with their status clearly shown as 'Enabled', or individual macro screens showing the 'Enable' toggle active.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Splunk application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the genuine Splunk Web UI or Splunk CLI being used, rather than a text editor or mock HTML file.

integrity.description: Agent did not just type the expected macro definitions into a text file or terminal without applying them to Splunk.
integrity.visual_evidence: The SPL queries and macro names are entered into the actual Splunk configuration fields (Web UI forms or macros.conf via terminal), not just printed to the screen.

integrity.description: Agent wrote distinct, functional SPL queries for each macro.
integrity.visual_evidence: The definitions for the three macros are different and match their specific requirements, rather than copy-pasting the exact same query for all three to bypass checks.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating three specific Splunk macros: 'auth_failures_by_ip(1)', 'top_attackers(1)', and 'event_volume_trend'. The target indices are 'security_logs', 'web_logs', and 'system_logs'. Macros 1 and 2 must query 'security_logs'. Macro 3 must query at least two of the three indices and use the 'timechart' command. Splunk credentials are admin / SplunkAdmin1!.
